content.text:
Excellent! I have all the information needed. Let me compile the complete findings:

## Dark Mode CSS Variables - Complete Report

### Main File: `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\index.css`

**Dark Mode Selector:** `[data-theme="dark"]` (Lines 74-132)

This is the primary location where all dark mode CSS variables are defined using the `[data-theme="dark"]` attribute selector.

### Dark Mode CSS Variable Definitions

**Background Colors:**
- `--bg-primary: #0C0C0C;` - Main dark background
- `--bg-secondary: #141414;` - Secondary dark surface
- `--bg-hover: #1E1E1C;` - Hover state background
- `--bg-elevated: #1A1A18;` - Elevated surface (modals, dropdowns)
- `--bg-card: #161614;` - Card background
- `--bg-backdrop: rgba(12,12,12,0.8);` - Backdrop overlay
- `--bg-backdrop-heavy: rgba(12,12,12,0.9);` - Heavy backdrop overlay
- `--bg-overlay: rgba(0,0,0,0.4);` - Overlay color
- `--bg-pill: rgba(255,240,210,0.06);` - Pill-shaped button background
- `--bg-glass: rgba(255,255,255,0.05);` - Glass morphism background
- `--bg-selection: rgba(212,168,83,0.1);` - Text selection background
- `--bg-selection-active: rgba(212,168,83,0.18);` - Active selection background

**Text Colors:**
- `--text-primary: #F0EBE3;` - Primary text (body text)
- `--text-heading: #F5F0E8;` - Heading text
- `--text-secondary: #B0A89E;` - Secondary text
- `--text-body: #908880;` - Body text alternative
- `--text-muted: #6B6560;` - Muted/disabled text
- `--text-faint: #504A45;` - Very faint text
- `--text-separator: #2E2A26;` - Separator/divider color

**Border Colors:**
- `--border-light: rgba(255,240,210,0.06);` - Light border
- `--border-card: rgba(255,240,210,0.04);` - Card border
- `--bg-glass-border: rgba(255,240,210,0.08);` - Glass morphism border

**Accent Colors:**
- `--accent-purple: #D4A853;` - Primary accent (amber/gold)
- `--accent-dark: #F0EBE3;` - Dark accent (light text)
- `--accent-dark-hover: #C8C0B5;` - Dark accent hover state
- `--accent-sage: #9AB08A;` - Sage/muted green accent
- `--accent-amber: #E0BA6A;` - Amber/gold accent

**Status Colors:**
- `--color-danger: #EF4444;` - Danger/error red
- `--color-danger-bg: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.12);` - Danger background
- `--color-success: #22C55E;` - Success green
- `--color-success-bg: rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.12);` - Success background

**Shadow/Visual Effects:**
- `--shadow-card: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);` - Card shadow
- `--shadow-card-sm: 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.25);` - Small card shadow
- `--shadow-modal: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);` - Modal shadow
- `--shadow-dropdown: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.4), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,240,210,0.04);` - Dropdown shadow

**Icon Colors:**
- `--icon-default: #908880;` - Default icon color
- `--icon-view: rgba(240,235,227,0.25);` - View icon (25% opacity)
- `--icon-view-count: rgba(240,235,227,0.55);` - View count icon (55% opacity)

**Typography:**
- `--font-serif: "Lora", Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif;` - Serif font
- `--font-ui: "Outfit", sans-serif;` - UI font

**Loader:**
- `--loader-color: #D4A853;` - Loader/spinner color

**Global Settings for Dark Mode:**
- `color-scheme: dark;` - Informs browser of dark color scheme
- `color: var(--text-primary);` - Text color
- `background-color: var(--bg-primary);` - Background color

### Additional Dark Mode Styling Files

Dark mode component-level overrides are found in multiple CSS files using `[data-theme="dark"]` selector:
- `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Analytics\AnalyticsDashboard.css`
- `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\DashboardBriefing\DashboardBriefing.css`
- `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\explore\explore.css`
- `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\postCards\ProfilePostCards\profilepostcards.css`
- `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\LoginPage\login.css`
- `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\ProfilePage\components\ProfileStoriesSection.css`
- `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\SettingsPage\SettingsPage.css`
- And many more component files

### Implementation Method

The project uses a **CSS variable + attribute selector** approach for theme switching. Dark mode is activated by setting the `data-theme="dark"` attribute on the root element (likely the `<html>` or a parent container). All theme-sensitive components reference the CSS custom properties defined in `:root` (light mode) and `[data-theme="dark"]` (dark mode), ensuring automatic color switching across the entire application.

input.new_string:
# Soften Dark Mode — Warm Charcoal Instead of Pitch Black

## Context
The current dark mode uses near-black backgrounds (`#0C0C0C`, `#141414`, etc.) which feel too harsh. Shift to a warm charcoal palette — still clearly dark mode but softer, similar to Notion/Linear.

## File to Modify
`client/src/index.css` — `[data-theme="dark"]` block (lines 74–132)

## Changes

Update background variables to warm charcoal tones:

| Variable | Current | New |
|---|---|---|
| `--bg-primary` | `#0C0C0C` | `#1A1916` |
| `--bg-secondary` | `#141414` | `#222220` |
| `--bg-hover` | `#1E1E1C` | `#2C2C2A` |
| `--bg-elevated` | `#1A1A18` | `#262624` |
| `--bg-card` | `#161614` | `#1E1E1C` |
| `--bg-backdrop` | `rgba(12,12,12,0.8)` | `rgba(26,25,22,0.85)` |
| `--bg-backdrop-heavy` | `rgba(12,12,12,0.9)` | `rgba(26,25,22,0.92)` |
| `--bg-glass` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.05)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.07)` |
| `--text-separator` | `#2E2A26` | `#3A3632` |

Also bump border/glass opacity slightly for better visibility on lighter surfaces:

| Variable | Current | New |
|---|---|---|
| `--border-light` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.06)` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.08)` |
| `--border-card` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.04)` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.06)` |
| `--bg-glass-border` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.08)` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.10)` |

Soften shadows (less contrast needed on lighter dark bg):

| Variable | Current | New |
|---|---|---|
| `--shadow-card` | `0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)` | `0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.2)` |
| `--shadow-card-sm` | `0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.25)` | `0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)` |
| `--shadow-modal` | `0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.5)` | `0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.35)` |
| `--shadow-dropdown` | `0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.4), ...` | `0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.25), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,240,210,0.06)` |

Text, accent, and icon colors stay unchanged — they already have good contrast against charcoal.

## Verification

toolUseResult.newString:
# Soften Dark Mode — Warm Charcoal Instead of Pitch Black

## Context
The current dark mode uses near-black backgrounds (`#0C0C0C`, `#141414`, etc.) which feel too harsh. Shift to a warm charcoal palette — still clearly dark mode but softer, similar to Notion/Linear.

## File to Modify
`client/src/index.css` — `[data-theme="dark"]` block (lines 74–132)

## Changes

Update background variables to warm charcoal tones:

| Variable | Current | New |
|---|---|---|
| `--bg-primary` | `#0C0C0C` | `#1A1916` |
| `--bg-secondary` | `#141414` | `#222220` |
| `--bg-hover` | `#1E1E1C` | `#2C2C2A` |
| `--bg-elevated` | `#1A1A18` | `#262624` |
| `--bg-card` | `#161614` | `#1E1E1C` |
| `--bg-backdrop` | `rgba(12,12,12,0.8)` | `rgba(26,25,22,0.85)` |
| `--bg-backdrop-heavy` | `rgba(12,12,12,0.9)` | `rgba(26,25,22,0.92)` |
| `--bg-glass` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.05)` | `rgba(255,255,255,0.07)` |
| `--text-separator` | `#2E2A26` | `#3A3632` |

Also bump border/glass opacity slightly for better visibility on lighter surfaces:

| Variable | Current | New |
|---|---|---|
| `--border-light` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.06)` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.08)` |
| `--border-card` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.04)` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.06)` |
| `--bg-glass-border` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.08)` | `rgba(255,240,210,0.10)` |

Soften shadows (less contrast needed on lighter dark bg):

| Variable | Current | New |
|---|---|---|
| `--shadow-card` | `0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)` | `0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.2)` |
| `--shadow-card-sm` | `0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.25)` | `0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)` |
| `--shadow-modal` | `0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.5)` | `0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.35)` |
| `--shadow-dropdown` | `0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.4), ...` | `0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.25), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,240,210,0.06)` |

Text, accent, and icon colors stay unchanged — they already have good contrast against charcoal.

## Verification
- Toggle dark mode in browser and check: home feed, profile page, editor, modals, dropdowns
- Ensure text remains readable (WCAG contrast should improve slightly)
- Cards/elevated surfaces should be visually distinct from the background
